title: NASA will wear high-tech Prada long johns to the Moon
summary: The Artemis program will equip astronauts with Prada-designed liquid cooling garments to wear under spacesuits during the 2028 Moon mission. These high-tech long johns circulate cold water through embedded tubes to regulate body temperature during spacewalks, with a backup system for redundancy. This advancement makes lunar exploration more comfortable and safer for crews.
content:
We've seen Axiom Space and Prada's collaboration on the Axiom Extravehicular Mobility Unit (AxEMU) spacesuit. Now the company has revealed the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG) that astronauts will wear underneath it when Artemis IV returns humans to the Moon in 2028.
The LCVG is the all-important base layer that will keep the crew cool and comfortable while inside the AxEMU and on spacewalks. Cold water is circulated through tubes embedded in the suit to whisk heat away from astronauts' bodies. And, should the primary system fail, there is a backup, unlike older cooling suits. The LCVG also houses the ventilation system that su …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: NASA宇航员将穿着高科技普拉达长内衣登月
zh.summary: 阿尔忒弥斯计划将为宇航员配备普拉达设计的液冷装置,在2028年登月任务中穿在宇航服下。这些高科技长内衣通过内置管道循环冷水来调节太空行走中的体温,配有备用系统以实现冗余。这项进步使月球探索对机组人员更加舒适和安全。

title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?

This shift reflects the pressure these companies face to demonstrate profitability to investors and the potential cost implications for businesses relying on AI infrastructure.
content: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

title: The 7 biggest storylines from Summer Game Fest 2026
summary: The 2026 Summer Game Fest showcased major publishers' shift back to exclusive games and single-player blockbusters amid industry challenges. Sony and Microsoft dominated with major announcements about their core strategies, while most publishers avoided competing with Grand Theft Auto VI's upcoming release. The event highlighted both industry strength and efforts to navigate current market headwinds.
content:
The 2026 edition of Summer Game Fest just wrapped up, and it was surprisingly hectic. The nearly week-long event came at a challenging time for the games industry, and for the most part the big keynotes were used as a chance to show some strength through major announcements, while largely ignoring pesky details like hardware price hikes and the mess of the live-service space.
Both Sony and Microsoft used their showcases as a way to confirm they're refocusing on tried-and-true strategies like exclusive games and single-player blockbusters. Meanwhile, every publisher in existence seemed to be avoiding going up against Grand Theft Auto VI on t …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 2026年Summer Game Fest的7大看点
zh.summary: 2026年的Summer Game Fest展示了主要发行商在行业挑战中转向独占游戏和单人大作的趋势。索尼和微软通过关于其核心战略的重大公告主导了活动，而大多数发行商避免与即将发行的侠盗猎车手VI竞争。该活动展现了行业的实力和应对当前市场挑战的努力。

title: Persona 6 exists, and that’s all we know
summary: Atlus confirmed Persona 6 at Summer Game Fest with a brief teaser suggesting a dark tone, committing to releases on PS5, Xbox, and PC. The new standalone entry promises to blend daily-life simulation with supernatural adventure elements.
content:
This year's Summer Game Fest turned out to be a good one for fans of Japanese RPGs. First, the conclusion of the Final Fantasy VII remake trilogy was announced at SGF Live, and now we have the long-awaited return of the Persona series.
Atlus confirmed Persona 6 exists with a brief teaser, but aside from implying a very dark tone, that's about all the video revealed. There's no release date or window, though the game will be coming to the PS5, Xbox, and PC whenever it does launch. Atlus describes the game as "a bold, new standalone story blending heartfelt daily life and new characters with pulse-pounding, supernatural adventure," which coul …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Will you and your allies prevail against the forces of evil and save the day once more?
Minecraft Dungeons 2 will be available on Nintendo Switch, Nin …

title: Fable launches in late February after recent delay
summary: The Fable reboot launches February 23, 2027, with early access for Premium Edition buyers starting February 18, marking the fantasy RPG franchise's return to new releases after 17 years.
content:
Just a few days after pushing Fable out of 2026, Microsoft showed off more footage of Fable, the first new entry in the storied RPG franchise since 2010's Fable III, at its Xbox Games Showcase on Sunday. The company also announced a specific release date: February 23rd, 2027. Though if you get the Premium Edition, you'll get early access starting February 18th.
The trailer from the showcase follows a dedicated gameplay overview shown earlier this year. Based on what Microsoft has shown so far, the new Fable looks like it could deliver on what people love about the original trilogy, including the fantasy RPG setting and a lot of British humo …

title: Halo: Campaign Evolved arrives July 28th
summary: Halo: Campaign Evolved releases July 28 across Xbox Series S/X, PC, and PlayStation 5, featuring a modern Unreal Engine 5 remake with new story content and 4K visuals.
content:
As part of its Xbox Games Showcase on Sunday, Microsoft revealed new details about Halo: Campaign Evolved, the upcoming remake of Halo: Combat Evolved's campaign mode. The remake will debut on Xbox Series S / X, PC, and PS5 on July 28th.
Today's mission trailer includes a first look at Operation: Meteorite, a new three-mission story arc in Halo: Campaign Evolved. It takes place a year before the events of the first game and features Master Chief and Sgt. Johnson.
Microsoft announced Campaign Evolved last year, calling it a "modern evolution" of the original story. The game is built in Unreal Engine 5 and features "stunning new 4K visuals,  …

title: Gears of War: E-Day isn’t coming to the PS5
summary: Microsoft confirmed Gears of War: E-Day will be exclusive to Xbox and PC, launching October 6 and ruling out a PlayStation 5 release despite earlier reports.
content:
Apparently, the "return of Xbox" means a retreat from other platforms. At its Xbox Games Showcase today, Microsoft revealed that Gears of War: E-Day - which was previously rumored for a PS5 launch in addition to Xbox and PC - will not be coming to PlayStation. It'll be an Xbox console exclusive and is launching on October 6th.
The announcement was preceded by a trailer, which opened with a street-level view of an alien attack, with the extraterrestrial invaders being fought off in an urban landscape, including an electronics store and grocery shop. It looked like familiar Gears gameplay: lots of blood and lots of cover. It also may have bee …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: The Verge Weekend Questionnaire
summary: The Verge's Weekend Questionnaire is a recurring Saturday interview feature profiling notable figures from tech, music, and politics on their work habits and technology preferences.
content:
Have you ever wondered what the most indispensable app is for your favorite musician or how the world’s tech CEOs stay focused? Well, that’s the sort of thing we aim to uncover in our Verge Weekend Questionnaire. Think of it as a spiritual successor to Five Minutes on the Verge.
Every Saturday, a different guest submits to our rapid-fire menu of questions, and we bring you their unfiltered answers. Subjects come from a variety of worlds. We talk to content creators, authors, politicians, developers… basically anyone interesting. We want to know how they get their work done, how they unplug, and, of course, what GIFs and memes they’re sending to their friends and loved ones.
Want to know what legendary designer insists on only opening one browser tab at a time and who holds the Game Boy up as their favorite gadget of all time? Check back each week for all that and more.


					
Benn Jordan longs for the days of tech that didn’t spy on you
			
Welcome to Night Vale host Cecil Baldwin shares his tech pet peeves
			
The man behind the legendary MPC, Roger Linn, stays focused with a single browser tab
			
NPR’s Manoush Zomorodi talks about living with too much tech

zh.title: The Verge周末问卷

title: Xbox Games Showcase 2026: All the news and trailers
summary: Microsoft's Xbox Games Showcase sent mixed signals on exclusivity strategy, making Gears of War exclusive while keeping major titles like Fable and Halo multiplatform under new leadership pursuing a 'return of Xbox.'
content:
The console industry is in a weird place, and both Xbox and PlayStation have a chance to change the narrative a bit with their showcases at Summer Game Fest. Sony did that by focusing on the single-player titles it’s known for, and then it was Microsoft’s turn.
The Xbox Games Showcase was focused mainly on games we already know, but there was also some confused messaging about the company’s exclusivity strategy. Gears of War: E-Day won’t be launching on the PS5, which was a big surprise, but other major first-party titles like Fable and Halo: Combat Evolved are still coming to Sony’s console. That makes it hard to tell just how focused Microsoft really is on exclusives.
In a blog post recapping the event, the company said that “Games already announced for multiplatform releases will stick to that plan — we’re committed to investing in and growing Xbox both on console and beyond,” suggesting that future first-party titles will be only for Xbox consoles.
The event came at an interesting time for Xbox. Microsoft’s gaming division went through an executive shake-up in April, and new boss Asha Sharma has been making some smaller but welcome changes, while also promising what she calls the “return of Xbox.” Given how confused the Xbox brand has been of late, it seems likely bigger changes are on the way.
There were at least a few surprises, though, including a first look at Sony’s Crazy Taxi revival and a very brief teaser for Persona 6.
You can follow all of our coverage from the Xbox showcase right here.


					
A return of Xbox console exclusives.
			
Another look at Call of Duty.
			
Clockwork Revolution is also an Xbox console exclusive.
			
Persona 6 exists, and that’s all we know
			
Spyro’s back.
			
A spooky new Castlevania for October.
			
Fly over some national parks.
			
Minecraft Dungeons 2 gets a September release date
			
Crazy Taxi is going on tour.
			
The DLC for Doom: The Dark Ages adds a throwable spear.
			
Back to hell.
			
Bad Magpie looks like Unti

title: Govee’s all-weather smart lamp post is under $200 for the first time
summary: Govee's all-weather outdoor smart lamp post, featuring 2,000 lumens and RGB lighting with motion detection, is discounted 23% to $199.99 and works with Matter for smart home integration.
content:
A look at how Jennifer’s yard gets a brightness boost thanks to Govee’s smart outdoor lamp post. | Photo: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy / The Verge	

If you have an outdoor space that needs some extra lighting, either for fun or security reasons (or a mix of the two), the Govee outdoor lamp post light is a good pick that’s 23 percent off its regular price right now. Typically retailing for $260, the Govee outdoor lamp post light is available for $199.99 from Amazon or directly from Govee — the lowest price we’ve seen. The lights cover a 360-degree spread, letting you blast its beams in a single color, or mix up the colors as you please. 
Govee Outdoor Lamp Post
A 8-foot-tall, 2,000-lumen RGBIC smart outdoor lamp. The model has six color zones and dedicated white lighting. It features built-in motion and light sensors, works with Matter, is IP66 rated, and includes a long power cord for easy installation. 

Where to Buy:

 $259.99 $199.99 at Amazon
 $259.99 $199.99 at Govee

Govee claims it can shine up to an impressive 2,000 lumens at peak brightness, and can illuminate up to 32 feet. In addition to full RGB support across six zones, the outdoor lamp post light has an adjustable color temperature (2700K to 6500K), with brightness automatically adjusting itself throughout the day thanks to a built-in sensor. Sensors can also be set to welcome guests by turning on when it detects them, or it can warn intruders by flashing red when triggered.
The Govee outdoor lamp post light has a IP66 rating, meaning that they should be able to withstand extreme weather. The lamp post includes multiple permanent installation options, as well as a fillable water bag as a temporary solution in case you want to move the lights around or pack them up.

title: AI ‘content creators’ are getting harder to spot
summary: AI-generated content creators are becoming increasingly difficult to distinguish from human influencers as technology advances. While early AI avatars were obviously artificial, newer generations are blurring the line between digital and human creators. This raises questions about authenticity verification and the need for clearer disclosure standards on social media.
content:
Aitana Lopez, AI avatar by creative agency The Clueless. | Image: The Clueless	

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on AI confusion, follow Robert Hart. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.
How it started 
At first, AI influencers were relatively easy to identify - and to ignore. Aside from the occasional bursts of hype, they didn't seem to change much about the way social media worked. The earliest virtual influencers - Lil Miquela with her blunt fringe and freckles, Imma with her bubblegum pink bob, and Shudu Gram with her flawless complexion - were obviously digital productions. Collaborations  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: AI "内容创作者"变得越来越难以识别

title: JMGO’s N3 Ultimate projector is the new portable 4K champ
summary: JMGO's N3 Ultimate is a portable 4K projector that excels at flexible placement and handling moderate ambient light, outperforming competing models like Anker's offerings. Priced at $2,399, the projector can rival more expensive home theater systems and justifies its premium cost through superior adaptability. The device represents a new benchmark for flagship portable projection technology.
content:
The N3 Ultimate doesn’t mind being off center. | Photo by Thomas Ricker / The Verge	

Sorry Anker: JMGO now makes my favorite flagship portable projector. 
The N3 Ultimate is an excellent portable 4K projector that defeats moderate ambient light at severe placement angles and can rival more expensive home theater installations at night. After a few weeks of testing, I think the raw adaptability exhibited by the JMGO's N3 Ultimate justifies its current $2,399 price ($500 off its $2,999 list).
Modern all-in-one projectors built around Google TV are already super accommodating when it comes to placement. Set one down on a living room table or campsite rock and it will begin searching for a screen or blank wall while avoiding o …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: JMGO N3 Ultimate投影仪是新一代便携式4K冠军
zh.summary: JMGO N3 Ultimate是一款便携式4K投影仪，在灵活放置和处理中等环境光线方面表现出色，超越了Anker等竞争对手的产品。价格为$2,399，该投影仪可以与更昂贵的家庭影院系统相媲美，其卓越的适应性为其高价格提供了正当理由。该设备代表了旗舰便携式投影技术的新基准。
